title: Grant ON nonexistent TABLE ENGINE works
description:
### Company or project name
CH Inc
### Describe what's wrong
If you GRANT an nonexistent table engine to a user or a role the query will succeeded.
```
GRANT TABLE ENGINE ON RandomName34343 TO test WITH GRANT OPTION
Query id: 7c0dbf11-26b7-488b-a85d-bb62be5250bf
Ok.
0 rows in set. Elapsed: 0.001 sec.
```
This is problem if, for example, you use a slightly wrong name, like using a full upper case name as the query will work but the grant won't:
```
GRANT TABLE ENGINE ON FILELOG TO test WITH GRANT OPTION;
GRANT TABLE ENGINE ON FILELOG TO test WITH GRANT OPTION
Query id: b746713e-3c94-4e19-880c-a8d96d0779eb
Ok.
0 rows in set. Elapsed: 0.001 sec.
```
```
production-01 :) CREATE TABLE t111111 (c0 Nullable(Int)) ENGINE = FileLog('CSV', '/etc/passwd');
CREATE TABLE t111111
(
`c0` Nullable(Int)
)
ENGINE = FileLog('CSV', '/etc/passwd')
Query id: ec58a913-821b-41f4-b6ed-081af1a6102e
Elapsed: 0.001 sec.
Received exception from server (version 25.9.1):
Code: 497. DB::Exception: Received from clickhouse-01:49000. DB::Exception: test: Not enough privileges. To execute this query, it's necessary to have the grant TABLE ENGINE ON FileLog. (ACCESS_DENIED)
```
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
See above
### Expected behavior
I expect the server should reject the query if the `TABLE ENGINE` does not exist. Keeping it or ignoring it on secondary services (or when starting up) is fine in case it was created without these checks (or in a different version with different engines), but not when running the query for the first time.
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table1 (id INT, name VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE test_table2 (value DOUBLE, description VARCHAR);
GRANT SELECT ON test_table2 TO user2;
SELECT * FROM test_table1 WHERE id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_table1);
SELECT 'NonExistentEngine' AS engine_name WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT * FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name = 'NonExistentEngine');
key_bug_features: GRANT on nonexistent table engine succeeds
